title: Leptoglossis acutiloba
taxonomy: Leptoglossis acutiloba is classified within the family Plantaginaceae. It belongs to the order Lamiales and the class Magnoliopsida. This classification places Leptoglossis acutiloba within the angiosperm group, which is characterized by flowering plants. The genus Leptoglossis is a member of the tribe Veroniceae, further specifying the plant's classification within the Plantaginaceae family. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships with other species within the same family and order.

intro: Leptoglossis acutiloba is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae, commonly known as a type of billet root or wild basil. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of digestive issues, inflammation, and pain relief, as well as its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Leptoglossis acutiloba include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Leptoglossis acutiloba is often prepared as a decoction or infused as a tea, as well as being used in tinctures and capsules for its medicinal benefits.
